name: p0-geyser-thermostat-intermittent
description: Hot water from the geyser is intermittent. Some showers are hot for an hour, others go lukewarm halfway through. I took a photo of the thermostat dial on the side of the geyser but the photo is pretty blurry — the indicator is somewhere between 50 and 65, hard to tell exactly.

hypotheses.label: Thermostat set too low for household demand
evidence_for: Intermittent hot-water symptom is consistent with under-set thermostat
Dial appears to be sitting in the lower half of its range
evidence_against: Photo too blurry to read the actual setting

hypotheses.label: Failing thermostat tripping under load
evidence_for: Hot-then-lukewarm pattern matches a thermostat cutting out before recovery
evidence_against: Dial position itself does not confirm electrical failure

hypotheses.label: Sediment buildup reducing usable hot volume
evidence_for: Reduced hot-water duration is a classic sediment symptom
evidence_against: No information yet about geyser age or last service

mockAgentOutput.what_we_dont_know: What temperature the thermostat dial is actually set to — the photo is too blurry to read.
mockAgentOutput.why_it_matters: If the dial is at 50°C, the fix is to raise the setting; if it's at 65°C, the thermostat itself is likely failing under load and needs replacing.

chips.text: Take a clearer photo of the thermostat dial.
The dial is set close to 50.
The dial is set close to 65 or higher.

expected.must_ask_about: what the thermostat dial actually reads

failure_baseline_today.observed_behaviour: System asks a generic 'tell us more about your hot water problem' clarification — not a specific question targeting the dial reading.
failure_baseline_today.why_it_fails: Agent 2c's discriminator is not tied to the specific blurry-image signal. The prompt has no instruction to identify and target the dominant uncertainty source (the dial).

expected.phase_6_ask_rationale: Top hypothesis confidence_alone=0.55 (<0.75) and gap to second=0.10 (<0.25). A discriminating chip targeting the dial reading meaningfully reduces uncertainty — so ask, not commit_low_confidence.
